narrativeMd:
# IPO Forensic Analysis — PT Prodia Diagnostic Line Tbk (Ticker: **PRDL** / "Proline")

> **Source document:** *Prospektus Awal* (Preliminary Prospectus), 348 pages, issued 18 June 2026. FY2025 financials audited by **KAP Purwanto Susanti dan Surja (member firm of Ernst & Young Global / EY Indonesia)**, **unmodified opinion** with a **Key Audit Matter (Hal Audit Utama) on REVENUE RECOGNITION**. FY2024/2023 comparatives were audited by a **different firm ("KAP HHES")** and **restated/reissued** (Emphasis of Matter). See §6 and §7.4 — the auditor change + restatement cluster is a core quality item.
> **Analyst note (method):** preliminary prospectus → price is a *range*, issuance-cost lines are "•%". Where I quantify net proceeds, it is **back-solved from the prospectus's own allocation arithmetic**, not a heuristic — and clearly caveated. Figures are in Rupiah; the audited statements are presented "dalam ribuan Rupiah" (thousands), converted here to billions. Prepared for presentation use (Kang Ritel / kupas-prospektus). Opinions are labelled as analysis and attributed to the prospectus; no defamatory inference (UU ITE-aware).

---

## 0. Executive Snapshot

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| Issuer | PT Prodia Diagnostic Line Tbk — brand **Proline** (founded 16 Apr 2010 as *PT Dialine Systems Indonesia*; renamed 2011) |
| Ticker | **PRDL** (IDX), sector Healthcare / **In-Vitro Diagnostics (IVD)** manufacturing (KBLI 21015) |
| Core business | Makes IVD **reagents & instruments** (clinical chemistry, hematology, immunology, biomolecular) under *Proline*/*DiaSys* brands; sells mainly via third-party distributors |
| Shares offered | **522,900,000 new shares** = **30.00%** of post-IPO capital (100% primary) |
| Price range | **Rp100 – Rp120** per share (par **Rp50**) |
| Gross proceeds | **Rp52.29 bn (floor) – Rp62.748 bn (cap)** |
| Implied net proceeds | **≈ Rp57.0 bn at cap** (back-solved from the prospectus's own use-of-proceeds math → implied *biaya emisi* ≈ 9.2% of gross; see §3.1 — caveated) |
| Post-IPO market cap | **Rp174.3 bn – Rp209.1 bn** (1,742,900,000 shares) |
| Sole lead underwriter | **PT Sucor Sekuritas** — *full commitment* (same house running JELI; PRDL lists 9 Jul, JELI 7 Jul 2026) |
| Controlling shareholder | **PT Prodia Utama 51%** → 35.70% post-IPO |
| Other pre-IPO holders | **PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA — a *listed* company) 39% → 27.30%**; **Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH (Germany) 10% → 7.00%** |
| Ultimate Beneficial Owners | Six individuals (Prodia founders), each 16.67% of Prodia Utama: **Andi Widjaja, Gunawan Prawiro Soeharto, Haneke Suryadi, Justini Hidajat, Elisabeth Listijani W., Arjati Utami** |
| Lock-up | **8-month POJK 25/2017 lock-up on ALL three pre-IPO holders** + 12-month control commitment from the six UBOs — *stronger than JELI* |
| ESA | **Yes** — up to 36,603,000 shares (7% of offered), free ("cuma-cuma") to permanent staff, paid from internal cash, **12-month lock-up** |
| Dividend policy | Up to **20% payout** (lower than JELI's 30%) |
| Subsidiaries | **None** — standalone single entity |
| Indicative timeline | Bookbuilding 18–23 Jun · OJK effective 29 Jun · Public offer 1–7 Jul · Allotment 7 Jul · Listing **9 Jul 2026** |
| 2025 revenue / net profit | Rp74.37 bn (**+26.8% YoY**) / Rp16.99 bn (**+69.9% YoY**) — *but both far below the 2023 peak* |

**One-line thesis:** A small (Rp62 bn raise), founder-controlled IVD manufacturer inside the Prodia group, IPO-ing **primarily to repay bank debt (~63% of proceeds)** after a debt-funded capacity build collided with a **47% revenue collapse in 2024**. The 2023 "boom" was **~77% driven by a single BUMN distributor** (a government-channel order that did not repeat). Priced **cheaply in absolute terms (~10–12× trailing P/E, ~1.4× P/BV)** — appropriately, given extreme earnings volatility, customer concentration, a 2024 restatement, and a debt-repair use of proceeds. Real positives: EY audit, a genuine 8-month lock-up, healthy gross margins (61–68%), the Prodia brand/parentage, the Diasys technology licence (extended to 2033), and a structural government-health-spending tailwind (APBN 2026 Rp244 tn; free-health-check program).

---

## 1. Current Shareholders & Ownership Chain

### 1.1 Pre-IPO cap table (Akta No. 40, 6 Feb 2026; par Rp50)

| Shareholder | Shares | Nominal (Rp) | % |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **PT Prodia Utama** | 622,200,000 | 31,110,000,000 | **51.00%** |
| **PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA — listed)** | 475,800,000 | 23,790,000,000 | **39.00%** |
| **Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH (Germany)** | 122,000,000 | 6,100,000,000 | **10.00%** |
| **Total** | **1,220,000,000** | **61,000,000,000** | **100.00%** |

Authorised capital is 4,880,000,000 shares (Rp244 bn). A par-value re-split to Rp50 and the current structure were set up in Feb 2026 for the IPO. *(Founding 2010 structure was different — Drs. Soesanto Natanael 45.2%, Dr. Hubertus Susilaganda Sutoyo 20%, PRDA 34.8%; the original individuals were bought out and Prodia Utama + Diasys came in.)*

### 1.2 Post-IPO cap table (cap price)

| Shareholder | Shares | % |
|---|---:|---:|
| PT Prodia Utama | 622,200,000 | **35.70%** |
| PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA) | 475,800,000 | **27.30%** |
| Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH | 122,000,000 | 7.00% |
| **Public (<5% each)** | 522,900,000 | **30.00%** |
| **Total** | **1,742,900,000** | **100.00%** |

### 1.3 Ownership chain & UBOs

```
Six individuals (Prodia founders), 16.6667% each of PT Prodia Utama:
  Andi Widjaja · Gunawan Prawiro Soeharto · Haneke Suryadi · Justini Hidajat · Elisabeth Listijani W. · Arjati Utami
        │  (collectively control)
        ▼
PT Prodia Utama  ──51%──┐
PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA, listed) ──39%──┼──►  PT Prodia Diagnostic Line (PRDL)  [no subsidiaries]
Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH (Germany) ──10%──┘
```

- **No single dominant controller** — Prodia Utama is owned in six equal 16.67% slices. Control is exercised collectively by the founder group (declared beneficial owners under Perpres 13/2018). Andi Widjaja (Dr. Andi Wijaya, the Prodia founder) is Komisaris Utama of Prodia Utama.
- **The same six individuals also control listed PRDA** (via Prodia Utama). So PRDL is effectively a **second listing from the same controlling group** — a group monetisation/restructuring, not an independent company.

### 1.4 Board
Read the full director/commissioner list in Bab VIII; the prospectus appoints an Independent Commissioner per POJK 33/2014. *(Verify the named Independent Commissioner's genuine independence given the tight founder control — same governance caveat as any closely-held IDX issuer.)*

---

## 2. Affiliations with the Existing Stock Market & Related Parties

This is the densest part of the PRDL story — and it cuts **both ways**.

### 2.1 Affiliation with a *listed* company (PRDA)
- **PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA)** — a company **already listed on IDX** (clinical-laboratory operator) — owns **39% pre-IPO / 27.30% post-IPO** of PRDL, and shares the same ultimate controllers (via Prodia Utama). This is a **direct affiliation with an existing listed issuer**. Implications: (i) cross-read PRDA's disclosures and trading history for context; (ii) potential for related-party flows between PRDL and the PRDA lab network; (iii) the group has prior public-market experience (a mild positive for disclosure discipline).
- **PT Prodia Utama** is the common parent of both PRDA and PRDL — a classic group "carve-out" listing.

### 2.2 The Diasys relationship (shareholder + licensor + supplier — triple dependency)
- **Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH** (Germany): 10% pre / 7% post-IPO shareholder, **AND** the **technology licensor**, **AND** a **reagent/raw-material supplier**.
- Licence & cooperation agreement since **April 2011**: PRDL is licensed to manufacture reagents under Diasys supervision, using the "DiaSys" trademark (or "Proline" for reagents made with Diasys-supplied raw materials), with training, QC inspection and method updates from Diasys; PRDL pays licence fees. Original term to 8 Jun 2026; **extended 13 Apr 2026 to 17 Apr 2033**.
- ⚠️ **Dependency flag:** PRDL's core reagent technology rests on a single foreign related party that is also a shareholder. The recent extension (to 2033) de-risks the near term, but a non-renewal/dispute at term, or a change in Diasys's terms, is a structural risk. The related-party trade *payable* to Diasys (in Euro, Rp628 m at YE2025) confirms ongoing reagent purchases. Working-capital IPO proceeds may partly flow to Diasys (related party) for raw materials — disclosed as an affiliated transaction under POJK 42/2020.

### 2.3 Other related parties (mostly on the COST side — important)
Related-party **revenue is immaterial** (related-party trade receivables were only **2.0% (2023) / 3.7% (2024) / 7.6% (2025)** of total trade receivables). The Prodia network is **not** a captive sales channel. Related parties identified:

| Related party | Relationship | Nature of transactions |
|---|---|---|
| Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH | Shareholder | Inventory purchases, freight, **licence fee** |
| PT Prodia Distribusi Medika | Other related party | Sales & purchases of inventory; rental income; *other receivable Rp1.49 bn (99% of other receivables) in 2025* |
| PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA) | Other related party / shareholder | Sales & delivery of services; other operating expense |
| PT Innovasi Diagnostika | Other related party | Sales & purchases of inventory |
| PT Grhanis Prima Propertindo | Other related party | **Building maintenance expense** |
| PT Grhanis Putra Propertindo | Other related party | **Building rental expense** |

> **Read-through:** PRDL **rents its building from a related party** (Grhanis) and **buys core reagents/licence from a related party** (Diasys), but **sells mostly to third parties**. That is structurally *cleaner* on the revenue side than a captive intra-group model (less transfer-pricing/circular-revenue concern) — but it concentrates the risk in the third-party distributor channel (§3 red flags) and leaves cost-side related-party dependencies (Diasys, Grhanis) to monitor.

### 2.4 Capital-market affiliation of underwriter/professionals
- **PT Sucor Sekuritas** (sole lead, full commitment) — the same house running the JELI IPO two days earlier. Confirm the prospectus's affiliation declaration (Bab XII–XIII) states no affiliation with the issuer.

---

## 3. Public Offered Shares, Proceeds & Red Flags

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Shares offered | 522,900,000 (all **new/primary**) |
| % of post-IPO capital | 30.00% |
| Price range | Rp100 – Rp120 (par Rp50) |
| **Gross proceeds** | **Rp52.29 bn → Rp62.748 bn** |
| Selling shareholders | None |
| Greenshoe | None disclosed |
| Issuance-cost % | "•%" (blank in draft) — but net is back-solvable, see §3.1 |

### 3.1 Proceeds & Net (back-solved from the prospectus's own math)
Unlike JELI, PRDL's use-of-proceeds lets us **derive net proceeds from disclosed figures** rather than estimate: item 1 (debt) is a **fixed Rp35,666,666,667**, and items 2–3 are **28.92% + 8.51% = 37.43% of net**. Therefore debt = 62.57% of net → **net proceeds ≈ Rp35.667 bn ÷ 0.6257 ≈ Rp57.0 bn**. Against gross of Rp62.748 bn (cap), that implies **biaya emisi ≈ Rp5.75 bn ≈ 9.2% of gross** — high, but plausible for a sub-Rp65 bn deal where fixed costs (legal/audit/listing/printing) are a larger percentage.

> **Caveat (state on slide):** this back-solve assumes the percentages were computed at the **cap-price** scenario. At the **floor** (gross Rp52.29 bn), if rupiah costs are similar (~Rp5.75 bn), net ≈ Rp46.5 bn — and because **debt repayment is a fixed Rp35.67 bn**, it would absorb **~76.6% of net**, squeezing capex+WC to ~Rp11 bn. **Price sensitivity is severe** and asymmetric: a weak book hits the *growth* portion hardest.

### 3.2 Red flags / structuring notes
1. 🔴 **This is a debt-repair IPO, not a growth IPO.** ~63% of net proceeds (Rp35.67 bn) repays bank debt (BCA fully + ~Rp14.2 bn of Panin). Only ~28.9% capex + ~8.5% working capital funds growth. Investors are substantially **bailing out a leveraged balance sheet**, not funding expansion.
2. 🔴 **Even post-IPO the company stays leveraged.** Total bank debt at YE2025 ≈ **Rp94.3 bn**; repaying Rp35.67 bn leaves **~Rp58 bn outstanding** (mostly Panin). This is *partial* deleveraging.
3. 🟠 **Tiny float in rupiah terms** (Rp52–63 bn) at a **near-par price (Rp100–120 vs Rp50 par)** — the classic IDX small-cap "gocap-adjacent" profile, which the prospectus itself flags via **illiquidity as the secondary risk**. Expect thin liquidity and high aftermarket volatility (auto-reject dynamics common in this segment).
4. 🟡 **Media data discrepancy:** some outlets (Kompas/Kontan) quoted the debt repayment as "~Rp33.66 bn"; the prospectus states **Rp35,666,666,667**. Use the prospectus figure.

---

## 4. Lock-up, ESOP & ESA

### 4.1 Lock-up — *materially stronger than JELI*
- **All three pre-IPO shareholders** (PRDA 19 Feb, Prodia Utama 20 Feb, Diasys 24 Feb 2026) signed **POJK 25/2017 statements not to sell/transfer ANY shares for 8 months** after the effective date. So unlike JELI, there **is a hard 8-month lock-up on 100% of pre-IPO shares** (70% of post-IPO capital).
- Additionally, the **six individual UBOs** (Andi Widjaja, Gunawan Prawiro Soeharto, Justini Hidajat, Elisabeth Listijani W., Arjati Utami, Haneke Suryadi) committed (Apr 2026) **not to relinquish control for 12 months**.
- *Verify in Bab XVII whether the 8-month statement is the statutory POJK 25/2017 lock-up (triggered by below-IPO-price acquisitions within 6 months pre-registration) or a broader voluntary undertaking; either way, the practical effect — no founder selling for 8 months — is a genuine positive vs. JELI's no-hard-lock structure.*

### 4.2 ESA (Employee Stock Allocation) — exists, and it's a cash cost
- Up to **36,603,000 shares (7% of offered)**, exercise price = offer price, **given free ("cuma-cuma") to permanent employees** (≥12 months' tenure as of 30 Jun 2026, good-standing, good performance), **paid by the company from internal cash** to the underwriter. **12-month lock-up** on ESA shares.
- 💸 **Cost to the company:** 36.6 m × Rp100–120 = **Rp3.66–4.39 bn** of internal cash — that is **~22–26% of 2025 net profit** distributed as an employee bonus, spread over ~55 permanent employees (~Rp67–80 m each). Generous; it is a real P&L/cash cost and modestly dilutive of the cash that could otherwise reduce debt. Note it favourably (alignment/retention) but quantify the cost.
- No separate MESOP/option program disclosed.

---

## 5. Use of IPO Proceeds & Red Flags

| # | Allocation | Basis | ~Rp (at cap, net≈57.0 bn) | Type |
|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 1 | **Repay bank debt** — BCA (Rp21.5 bn, fully) + Panin (~Rp14.2 bn) | **Fixed Rp35.67 bn** | Rp35.67 bn (~62.6% of net) | Deleveraging |
| 2 | Capex — production machines, calibration equipment, vehicles, software, production re-layout, **AHU for Biomolecular Lab** | 28.92% of net | ~Rp16.5 bn | Capex |
| 3 | Working capital — raw materials, product development, sales & marketing | 8.51% of net | ~Rp4.9 bn | Working capital |

**The BCA facility (being fully cleared):** Akta No. 54 (27 Sep 2023) — Kredit Lokal Rp10 bn + Investasi I Rp10.5 bn + Investasi II Rp19.5 bn (total Rp40 bn facility); outstanding Rp23.25 bn at YE2025; **7.75%/yr; maturity 2033**. Proceeds funded the **purchase of office, lab and warehouse production facilities in Cikarang** — i.e., the 2024 capacity build. The company will pre-pay Rp1.75 bn from internal cash before IPO, then Rp21.5 bn from proceeds → BCA balance Rp0.

### Red flags
1. 🔴 **Majority of fresh equity services legacy bank debt that financed an ill-timed, debt-funded capacity build** (non-current assets ~doubled from Rp66.6 bn in 2023 to Rp148.9 bn in 2025) **just as revenue halved in 2024**. The IPO is the de-risking exit from that mismatch.
2. 🟠 **Capex (item 2) expands capacity further** while utilization is undisclosed and the business is ~33% below its 2023 revenue peak — adding capacity into soft demand carries the same fixed-cost-drag risk flagged for JELI.
3. 🟡 The remaining ~Rp58 bn of bank debt post-IPO means **interest expense and covenant compliance remain live concerns** (covenants: EBITDA/(interest+installments) ≥1, current ratio ≥1, debt/equity ≤2 — all met at YE2025 at 2.81 / 2.14 / 1.34, but **debt/equity hit 1.78 in 2024**, close to the ≤2 limit).

## 6. Current Company Performance (Forensic Financial Review)

All figures Rp billion (converted from "ribuan Rupiah"). **Single entity — no consolidation, so net profit = owner profit (no NCI).**

### 6.1 Income statement — extreme volatility, COVID/government-order distortion

| Line | 2023 | 2024 | 2025 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **Revenue** | **111.78** | **58.66** | **74.37** |
| YoY growth | +301.9% | **−47.5%** | +26.8% |
| COGS | (35.30) | (24.12) | (29.02) |
| **Gross profit** | 76.47 | 34.54 | 45.35 |
| Gross margin | **68.4%** | 58.9% | 61.0% |
| Pre-tax profit | 47.14 | 13.38 | 22.52 |
| **Net profit (= owner profit)** | **35.78** | **10.00** | **16.99** |
| YoY growth | +1,809% | **−72.1%** | +69.9% |
| Net margin | 32.0% | 17.0% | 22.8% |
| **EPS (Rp, pre-IPO 1.22 bn sh)** | **29.32** | **8.19** | **13.93** |
| ROE | **63.7%** | 15.1% | 20.5% |

> 🚩 **The 2023 figure is an anomaly, not a baseline.** Revenue +301.9% (so 2022 was only ~Rp27.8 bn) and net profit +1,809% — then a 47.5%/72.1% collapse in 2024. The 2025 "recovery" (+26.8% revenue, +69.9% profit) is real but leaves the business **~33% below its 2023 revenue peak and ~53% below peak profit**. **Do not headline the +69.9% profit growth** without the 2023→2024 collapse beside it — the cherry-picked base flatters the story.

### 6.2 The volatility driver — single-distributor (BUMN) concentration
From the significant-agreements disclosure, revenue by major channel:

| Distributor / channel | 2023 | 2024 | 2025 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **PT Rajawali Nusindo (BUMN distributor)** | **Rp86.07 bn** | Rp25.58 bn | Rp24.49 bn |
| — as % of total revenue | **~77.0%** | ~43.6% | ~32.9% |
| PT Sumbermitra Agungjaya | Rp12.46 bn | Rp9.82 bn | Rp7.65 bn |
| PT Visi Trading (OEM) | — | Rp4.14 bn | Rp8.16 bn |
| **Top-3 named, 2025** | | | **~Rp40.3 bn ≈ 54%** |

> 🔴 **This is the single most important finding.** The 2023 boom was **~77% a single BUMN distributor (Rajawali Nusindo)** — almost certainly a government health-program channel order (JKN / disease-control / screening) that **did not repeat**. When it fell ~70% in 2024, total revenue halved. Even in 2025, Rajawali Nusindo is ~33% of revenue and the top-3 channels are ~54%. **Severe customer/channel concentration + dependence on lumpy government procurement** = the prospectus's own #1 stated risk ("ketergantungan terhadap belanja pemerintah pada sektor kesehatan"). Earnings are inherently low-visibility.

### 6.3 Cash flow & balance sheet — debt-funded build, then a cash drain

| | 2023 | 2024 | 2025 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Cash & equivalents | 28.66 | 29.46 | **10.80** |
| Total assets | 125.14 | 184.08 | 194.43 |
| — Non-current assets | 66.58 | 112.69 | **148.91** |
| Total liabilities | 68.96 | 117.90 | 111.37 |
| — incl. bank debt (≈) | | | **~94.3** |
| Total equity | 56.18 | 66.18 | 83.06 |
| Debt/Equity | 1.23 | **1.78** | 1.34 |
| Current ratio | 2.64 | 3.47 | 2.14 |
| DSCR | 10.44 | **1.95** | 2.81 |

- **Non-current assets ~doubled (Rp66.6 → 148.9 bn)** over 2023–25, **debt-funded** (long-term liabilities jumped from Rp46.7 bn in 2023 to Rp97.3 bn in 2024). The Cikarang facility build.
- **Cash collapsed 63% in 2025 (Rp29.5 → 10.8 bn)** — the company drew down liquidity servicing debt and funding the build into a soft-demand year. Low cash (Rp10.8 bn) is itself a reason the IPO/deleveraging is needed now.
- **DSCR fell to 1.95 in 2024** — thin debt-service cover, close to the covenant floor (≥1) and D/E (1.78) near the ≤2 limit. The IPO repayment restores headroom.

### 6.4 Audit quality — the cluster to scrutinise
- **FY2025:** audited by **EY Indonesia (KAP Purwanto Susanti dan Surja)** — Big-4, a step up from JELI's mid-tier auditor. Opinion **unmodified**. **Key Audit Matter = revenue recognition** (entirely appropriate given the volatility/concentration). The 2025 report was also **reissued** (Other Matter).
- **FY2024/2023:** audited by a **different firm ("KAP HHES")**, with a KAM on revenue recognition **and an Emphasis-of-Matter paragraph disclosing that the 2024 statements were RESTATED/REISSUED and 2023 comparatives re-presented.**
- ⚠️ **Two yellow-to-orange flags stacked:** (i) an **auditor change** right before IPO (HHES → EY), and (ii) a **restatement of 2024**. The restatement *reason* is not spelled out in the summary I reviewed — **this is the top diligence item.** It could be benign (reclassification, IPO-driven presentation alignment) or substantive (an error correction in the very revenue line that is the KAM). **Do not publish a clean "audited by EY" line without first reading the full EY/HHES reports and the restatement note** to establish what was restated and why.

## 7. Additional Useful Information

### 7.1 Business profile
- **IVD manufacturer (KBLI 21015)** under brand **Proline**: clinical chemistry, hematology, immunology and biomolecular **reagents + diagnostic instruments**. Core reagent tech licensed from **Diasys (Germany)** since 2011.
- **Customers:** 7,600+ end-users (hospitals, puskesmas, government & private clinical labs); distribution reaches **370 kabupaten/kota**. Management claims runway: ~47% of health facilities and ~38% of district health offices are **not yet** customers.
- **Distribution channels** are third-party distributors (Rajawali Nusindo — BUMN; Sumbermitra) and OEM (Visi Trading); also a quality agreement with Shenzhen Dymind (China) and supply relationships with Mindray, Robert Riele, MiCo BioMd, Maccura. A KFTD (Kimia Farma) distribution arrangement is also referenced.
- **Workforce:** small — **81 (2025)** vs 74 (2024) vs 70 (2023); 55 permanent / 26 contract.

### 7.2 Industry tailwind (the bull case)
- APBN 2026 health budget **Rp244 tn** (up from Rp218.5 tn in 2025). The government **Cek Kesehatan Gratis (CKG)** program (~Rp2.6 tn, 13 screening types) — PRDL targets **~Rp2.2 tn addressable** for 9 screening types it already produces. Structural diagnostics demand + import-substitution policy support is the genuine growth thesis. (It is also the source of the *concentration/lumpiness* risk — the same coin, two sides.)

### 7.3 Auditor / opinion → see §6.4. **EY (FY2025), restated FY2024 under prior auditor HHES.**

### 7.4 Dividend
- Policy: up to **20% of net profit** (vs JELI 30%), subject to RUPS; not legally binding. Given the post-IPO debt load and capex plans, **near-term cash dividends are likely minimal**. Confirm dividend history in Bab XI.

### 7.5 Legal & covenants
- Debt covenants met at YE2025 (DSCR 2.81 / current 2.14 / D-E 1.34); no covenant breaches or payment defaults disclosed; no negative covenants harmful to public shareholders. Confirm the litigation section (clean standing) in the legal opinion (Bab XVII).

### 7.6 Key risk factors (per prospectus)
1. **Primary: dependency on government health spending** (the cover's stated #1 risk) — directly maps to the Rajawali Nusindo/BUMN-channel concentration.
2. **Share illiquidity** (small float, near-par price, single underwriter).
3. Single-supplier/licensor dependency on **Diasys** (foreign, related party).
4. Raw-material/FX (EUR/USD reagent imports), regulatory (medical-device licensing, BPOM/Kemenkes), product-quality/recall risk.

> 💰 **Valuation verdict (analysis):** On an **absolute** basis PRDL is **cheap** (~10–12× trailing, ~1.4× book) — the opposite of JELI's premium. That discount is *earned*: extreme earnings volatility, single-channel/government concentration, a debt-repair use of proceeds, a 2024 restatement, and thin post-listing liquidity. The key valuation question is **what earnings power is normal** — anchoring on 2025 (Rp16.99 bn) gives ~10–12×; on a 3-yr average (~Rp20.9 bn) gives ~8–10×; the 2023 peak is not repeatable. **Relative verdict deferred:** the natural live comparable is **PRDA (its own listed affiliate)** plus other IDX healthcare/med-device names — pull their current multiples at publication date before asserting cheap/expensive *relative* to peers (no stale figures included here on purpose).

## 10. Bull vs. Bear

**Bull case**
- Real structural tailwind: rising government health budget (Rp244 tn) + CKG screening program (~Rp2.2 tn addressable); import-substitution policy favours a domestic IVD maker.
- Strong gross margins (61–68%); cheap absolute valuation (~10–12× P/E, ~1.4× book).
- Credible parentage (Prodia group, listed PRDA) + EY audit + Diasys technology (extended to 2033).
- **Genuine 8-month lock-up** on all founder shares (better than JELI); ESA aligns staff; post-IPO deleveraging restores covenant headroom.

**Bear case**
- **Debt-repair IPO**; earnings are **volatile and government/single-distributor dependent**; 2023 peak unrepeatable; business still below peak.
- **2024 restatement + auditor change** with revenue recognition as the KAM — unresolved.
- Capacity built ahead of demand; ~Rp58 bn debt remains; cash thin; tiny illiquid float.

---

## 11. Open Questions to Resolve Before Publishing
1. **Why was 2024 restated?** Read the EY and HHES reports + the restatement note. Was the revenue-recognition KAM the cause? This is the #1 item.
2. **Q1-2026 revenue/order book** — is the government channel (Rajawali Nusindo / CKG) re-accelerating, or is 2025's ~Rp74 bn the run-rate?
3. **Exact Panin facility terms** (size, rate, maturity) and the precise post-IPO residual debt and pro-forma interest expense.
4. **Final issuance-cost % / net proceeds** (confirm the back-solved ~Rp57 bn / ~9.2% once the final prospectus fills the "•%").
5. **Diasys economics** — licence-fee rate, raw-material pricing, exclusivity, and renewal terms to 2033.
6. **Utilization rate** of the new Cikarang capacity (undisclosed) and the realistic CKG-program revenue ramp.
7. **Live comparable multiples** (PRDA + IDX healthcare/med-device) at publication date for the relative-valuation call.

forensicMd:
## Thesis

RANS (PT RANS Entertainmen Indonesia Tbk) is a celebrity-IP media holding company built around Raffi Ahmad and Nagita Slavina, raising up to Rp429.25bn for 20.02% in a 100% primary offering. The bull case is a genuine, hard-to-replicate brand moat (one of Indonesia's largest social-media audiences) attached to a clean, almost debt-free balance sheet, with proceeds aimed at growth rather than repair. The bear case is in the trailing numbers: revenue has declined three years running and net profit fell 42% in 2025, while the deal is priced at a demanding ~28-35x earnings. This is a bet that the brand can re-accelerate a softening top line, paid for at a premium multiple, with the entire equity story resting on two people.

## How the business works

RANS monetises the audience and brand of Raffi Ahmad, Nagita Slavina and family across two audited segments. The Hiburan & Lifestyle segment (about 70% of FY2025 revenue and the profit engine, at roughly 35% segment margin) earns from social-media content and advertising, brand-ambassador and endorsement deals, talent management, a production house, and live events and ticketing. The Penjualan Produk segment (about 30%) sells IP-branded food, beverage and beauty products, and was loss-making in 2025 (a segment loss of around Rp12bn). The defining dependency is key-person concentration: revenue and brand value are tied to the continued involvement and reputation of Raffi and Nagita, which the issuer names as its own number-one risk. The demand backdrop is a large, young, social-first Indonesian consumer base, but the asset being sold is fundamentally the reach and goodwill of a single family.

## The offering & ownership

The IPO is 2,525,000,000 new shares (20.02% of post-IPO capital), entirely primary, so all proceeds accrue to the company. Pricing is Rp135 to Rp170 (par Rp10), implying a post-IPO market capitalisation of roughly Rp1.70tn to Rp2.14tn on 12,609,250,000 shares, underwritten on a full-commitment basis by Trimegah. Ownership is concentrated: Raffi Farid Ahmad holds 62.93% post-IPO as sole controller and ultimate beneficial owner, with the founder and family bloc (nine pre-IPO holders including Nagita Slavina, Dony Oskaria, Soultan Ariq Rachman and Kaesang Pangarep) at 79.98% against a 20.02% public float. The lock-up is genuinely strong: all nine pre-IPO holders signed 8-month statutory POJK 25/2017 lock-up statements, and Raffi has separately committed to retain control for three years. There is no ESA or MESOP programme. Dividend policy is up to 35% of net profit starting FY2026.

## Governance & related parties

Control and economic ownership are aligned in one person, which removes the control-mismatch question seen in some peers but raises the opposite concern: minority influence is minimal under a single ~63% controller. The use of proceeds carries the related-party item to watch. About 19.8% of the raise (Rp85bn) funds an acquisition tied to the Slavina/family brand, and a further slice funds an AI joint venture (Feedloop) and the Cipungland project, so a meaningful portion of public money flows into ventures within the founder's orbit; the arm's-length basis and independent valuation of these should be confirmed in the final prospectus. The board's independent-commissioner substance and the related-party framework around content and endorsement deals (where the controller is also the product) are the governance areas a careful reader should weigh. The issuer reports a clean legal record.

## Reading the financials

The trajectory is the crux. Revenue declined each of the last three years, from Rp437.8bn (2023) to Rp410.5bn (2024) to Rp353.4bn (2025). Reported net profit looks like it rose then fell sharply, but the 2024 figure is flattered: it includes a one-off Rp44.9bn gain on the disposal of a subsidiary (the RPKSB sports business), offset by a Rp10.5bn loss from that discontinued operation, leaving total profit for the year of Rp97.1bn. Strip the disposal out and the underlying earnings trend is softening rather than growing; 2025 total net profit of Rp56.7bn is down about 42%. Note that profit attributable to parent (Rp61.0bn in 2025) exceeds total profit because non-controlling interests absorbed losses, so valuation should use the parent figure. The balance sheet, by contrast, is a real strength: total liabilities of Rp120.2bn against equity of Rp340.8bn put DER at just 0.35x (down from 0.62x in 2023), interest cover is roughly 20x, and the only interest-bearing debt is a single BNI investment facility (about Rp37bn) that the IPO largely retires. This is a low-leverage, cash-generative business; the question is top-line direction, not solvency.

## Valuation

At the range, RANS prices at roughly 27.9 to 35.1x trailing FY2025 earnings attributable to parent (about 30 to 38x on total profit), with post-money price-to-book near 2.3 to 2.8x. That is a demanding multiple to pay for a company whose revenue has fallen three years running and whose 2024 profit was inflated by a non-recurring disposal. The multiple only makes sense if the brand-led growth plan (concerts, the Slavina acquisition, IP products, the AI venture) genuinely reignites the top line and the Product segment turns. Pre-money ROE of 16.6% dilutes to roughly 8% post-raise as the equity base expands. Note that issuance costs and the proforma equity table are blank in this preliminary draft, so net proceeds and exact post-money book value are back-solved and should be confirmed. A relative read needs live multiples for Indonesian media and IP peers at publication.

## Bottom line

RANS pairs a rare asset, a durable celebrity-IP brand on a clean, low-leverage balance sheet, with an awkward financial profile and a full price. The 100% primary, growth-oriented structure and the strong lock-up are points in its favour, and the deleveraging is almost incidental because there is little debt to repay. Against that sit three years of declining revenue, a 42% profit drop once the 2024 disposal gain is set aside, a loss-making product arm, related-party uses of proceeds, and a ~28-35x multiple. The entire thesis reduces to one question: can the Raffi and Nagita brand convert reach into re-accelerating, recurring revenue, and is that worth paying a premium for while the trailing line still points down. Underwrite it only if you believe the brand re-rates the growth, not the history.

ownership.summary: RANS is a founder-controlled media holding (Raffi Ahmad 62.93% post-IPO, with wife Nagita Slavina) carrying unusually dense political exposure: the controlling owner is himself a sitting Presidential Special Envoy, while the register also includes SOE regulatory chief Dony Oskaria and PSI chairman Kaesang Pangarep (son of ex-President Jokowi). It is simultaneously conglomerate-linked through Emtek/SCMA (PT Indonesia Entertainmen Grup ~7.2% plus Emtek CEO Sutanto Hartono) and the listed Bali United group. A celebrity-and-politically-connected float where governance, related-party and headline risk weigh heavily.

narrativeMd:
# RANS — PT RANS Entertainmen Indonesia Tbk

Celebrity-IP media holding built around Raffi Ahmad and Nagita Slavina. Upcoming IDX IPO (listing 10 Jul 2026), lead underwriter Trimegah.

- **Offering:** 2,525,000,000 new shares (100% primary) = 20.02% of post-IPO capital. Par Rp10, price Rp135–170, gross up to Rp429.25bn. Post-IPO shares 12,609,250,000.
- **Control:** Raffi Farid Ahmad 62.93% (sole controller & UBO); founder/family bloc 79.98%; public 20.02%. 8-month statutory lock on all 9 pre-IPO holders; 3-year control commitment. No ESA.
- **Business:** Hiburan & Lifestyle (~70% of revenue: content, endorsements, talent, production, events) + Penjualan Produk (~30%: IP-branded F&B and beauty, loss-making in 2025).
- **Financials (audited, full Rp):** Revenue 437.8bn (2023) → 410.5bn (2024) → 353.4bn (2025), declining three years. Net profit (total) 84.4 / 97.1 / 56.7bn; 2024 flattered by a Rp44.9bn one-off disposal gain. DER 0.62 / 0.36 / 0.35 — low leverage.
- **Use of proceeds:** growth-led — concerts 37.6%, Slavina acquisition 19.8%, Cipungland capex 18.6%, Feedloop AI JV 8.2%, BNI debt 7.0%, RNS subsidiary ~8.8%.
- **Valuation:** post-IPO cap Rp1.70–2.14tn; trailing P/E ~28–35x (parent); P/BV post-money ~2.3–2.8x; ROE 16.6% → ~8% post-money. Dividend up to 35% from FY2026.

See the structured cards and the full forensic writeup for the analysis. Figures from the preliminary prospectus (Prospektus Awal); issuance cost and proforma equity are blank in the draft, so net proceeds are back-solved.
